chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding the Circumambulation of the Uncircumcised

text: 2815 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ اَلَّذِي يُسْلِمُ فَيُرِيدُ أَنْ يَخْتَتِنَ وَ قَدْ حَضَرَ اَلْحَجُّ أَ يَحُجُّ أَوْ يَخْتَتِنُ قَالَ «لاَ يَحُجُّ حَتَّى يَخْتَتِنَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2815 - Ibn Maskan narrated from Ibrahim ibn Maymun, who reported from Imam Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who converts to Islam and intends to undergo circumcision while the time for Hajj has arrived.
He asked: Should he perform Hajj or undergo circumcision?
Imam (as) replied: "He should not perform Hajj until he is circumcised."
